name: KrillApp.DataPoint.Filter
title: Filters
description: A container node that organizes data filtering rules under a DataPoint. Does not filter data itself — actual filtering is performed by child filter nodes (DiscardAbove, DiscardBelow, Deadband, Debounce) during snapshot validation. All child filters must pass for a snapshot to be stored.
shortDescription: Container for filter nodes that validate incoming DataPoint snapshots.
llmPurpose: Organizes data filter child nodes under a DataPoint. Filters are evaluated during snapshot validation.
llmBehavior: child filters are evaluated during snapshot validation
all child filters must pass for data to be stored
llmInputs: none directly — children receive snapshots during validation
llmOutputs: none directly — children accept or reject snapshots
llmTypicalUseCases: add data quality filters to a data point
llmPromptHints: add a filter to this data point
llmCreationHints: Create a Filter node as a child of a DataPoint when the user wants to apply data quality or noise reduction rules. Then add specific filter types as children.

llmConnectionHints.role: intermediary container between DataPoint and specific filter implementations

llmExamples: Add filters to this data point to clean up noisy data.
Set up data quality rules for this sensor.
I want to filter out bad readings from this data point.
